body:
## Summary
- Makes `CountdownBanner` use the shared citizenship timeline phase helper so it stays visible between 6 June 2026 and the first civic-knowledge test sitting, then disappears afterward.
- Adds the missing focused `--focus-countdown-banner` validator route and summary counters advertised by the focus registry.

## Why
The browser countdown phase spec already covered the post-rules and post-first-sitting clocks, but the component still returned `null` after the rules date. UHR currently lists the first civic-knowledge test on 15 August 2026 and Stockholm-only August sitting details, while Migrationsverket lists the new rules from 6 June 2026.

## Validation
- `NODE_OPTIONS='--v8-pool-size=1' node scripts/validate-content.js --focus-countdown-banner`
- `NODE_OPTIONS='--v8-pool-size=1' node --test tests/content-countdown-banner-parity.test.js --test-name-pattern "countdown|civic test phase|post-launch"`
- `NODE_OPTIONS='--v8-pool-size=1' node --test tests/content-test-script-routing.test.js --test-name-pattern "countdown|focus-countdown"`
- `npm run typecheck -- --pretty false`
- `git diff --check`
- `CI=1 EXPO_NO_TELEMETRY=1 NODE_OPTIONS='--v8-pool-size=1' npm run build:web:export -- --max-workers 2`
- `CI=1 EXPO_NO_TELEMETRY=1 E2E_PORT=4175 NODE_OPTIONS='--v8-pool-size=1' npm run test:e2e -- tests/e2e/countdown-banner-source-affordance.spec.ts --workers=1`
- `NODE_OPTIONS='--v8-pool-size=1' npm run validate:content`
title: setup: render countdown civic-test phase
